perturbation.original_text: 4. Voting Commitments. The White Hat Parties shall appear in person or by proxy for quorum purposes at the 2020 Annual Meeting, including any adjournment or postponement thereof, to vote all of the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties on the record date (if the 2020 Annual Meeting is within thirty (30) days of the anniversary of the Company's 2019 Annual Meeting of Stockholders, such number of shares shall be no less than 90% of the Current Position) for such meeting (i) in favor of all persons nominated by the Board to serve as directors of the Company and against any stockholder nominated candidate not endorsed by the Board, (ii) against the stockholder proposal made pursuant to Rule 14a-8 under the Exchange Act as disclosed to the White Hat Parties prior to the execution of this Agreement, (iii) to ratify the appointment of the Company's independent registered public accounting firm, and (iv) in accordance with the Board's recommendation with respect to the Company's "say-on-pay" proposal and new equity compensation plan (collectively, the "2020 Proposals"), provided, that with respect to the proposals described in clauses (ii) through (iv) of this Section 4, the White Hat Parties may vote in accordance with the recommendation of Institutional Shareholders Services to the extent such recommendation differs from the voting commitments set forth herein and provided that the White Hat Parties do not publicly disclose their vote on such proposals if it differs from the Board's recommendation; and provided, further, that White Hat Parties shall have the right to vote the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties in their sole discretion with respect to all other proposals brought before the 2020 Annual Meeting. The White Hat Parties shall provide written evidence of the votes made in accordance with the foregoing sentence to the Company no later than ten business days before the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Voting Commitments. The White Hat Parties shall appear in person or by proxy for quorum purposes at the 2020 Annual Meeting, including any adjournment or postponement thereof, to vote all of the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties on the record date (if the 2020 Annual Meeting is within thirty (30) days of the anniversary of the Company's 2019 Annual Meeting of Stockholders, such number of shares shall be no less than 90% of the Current Position) for such meeting (i) in favor of all persons nominated by the Board to serve as directors of the Company and against any stockholder nominated candidate not endorsed by the Board, (ii) against the stockholder proposal made pursuant to Rule 14a-8 under the Exchange Act as disclosed to the White Hat Parties prior to the execution of this Agreement, (iii) to ratify the appointment of the Company's independent registered public accounting firm, and (iv) in accordance with the Board's recommendation with respect to the Company's "say-on-pay" proposal and new equity compensation plan (collectively, the "2020 Proposals"), provided, that with respect to the proposals described in clauses (ii) through (iv) of this Section 4, the White Hat Parties may vote in accordance with the recommendation of Institutional Shareholders Services to the extent such recommendation differs from the voting commitments set forth herein and provided that the White Hat Parties do not publicly disclose their vote on such proposals if it differs from the Board's recommendation; and provided, further, that White Hat Parties shall have the option to manage the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties as they see fit with respect to all other proposals brought before the 2020 Annual Meeting. The White Hat Parties shall provide written evidence of the votes made in accordance with the foregoing sentence to the Company no later than ten business days before the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'right to vote' to 'option to manage as they see fit' introduces ambiguity. While the original text commits the White Hat Parties to vote according to specific conditions, the modified text suggests they have an 'option to manage as they see fit,' potentially contradicting the earlier voting commitments, and creates uncertainty regarding the enforcement of the voting commitments within the text. It creates an In-Text Contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Voting Commitments. The White Hat Parties shall appear in person or by proxy for quorum purposes at the 2020 Annual Meeting, including any adjournment or postponement thereof, to vote all of the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties on the record date (if the 2020 Annual Meeting is within thirty (30) days of the anniversary of the Company's 2019 Annual Meeting of Stockholders, such number of shares shall be no less than 90% of the Current Position) for such meeting (i) in favor of all persons nominated by the Board to serve as directors of the Company and against any stockholder nominated candidate not endorsed by the Board, (ii) against the stockholder proposal made pursuant to Rule 14a-8 under the Exchange Act as disclosed to the White Hat Parties prior to the execution of this Agreement, (iii) to ratify the appointment of the Company's independent registered public accounting firm, and (iv) in accordance with the Board's recommendation with respect to the Company's "say-on-pay" proposal and new equity compensation plan (collectively, the "2020 Proposals"), provided, that with respect to the proposals described in clauses (ii) through (iv) of this Section 4, the White Hat Parties may vote in accordance with the recommendation of Institutional Shareholders Services to the extent such recommendation differs from the voting commitments set forth herein and provided that the White Hat Parties do not publicly disclose their vote on such proposals if it differs from the Board's recommendation; and provided, further, that White Hat Parties shall have the option to manage the shares of Common Stock beneficially owned by the White Hat Parties as they see fit with respect to all other proposals brought before the 2020 Annual Meeting. The White Hat Parties shall provide written evidence of the votes made in accordance with the foregoing sentence to the Company no later than ten business days before the 2020 Annual Meeting.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 4 commits the White Hat Parties to vote in a specific manner, but the changed text grants them the 'option to manage as they see fit' with respect to other proposals. This introduces a direct contradiction, as the 'option to manage as they see fit' implies discretion that conflicts with the prior commitment to vote according to specific conditions. This creates uncertainty regarding the enforcement of the voting commitments outlined earlier in the section and throughout the agreement, which depends on the definition of 'Voting Commitments' in the first sentence, thus creating an In-Text Contradiction under the definition of Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Support Period Covenants. Except as otherwise contemplated in this Agreement, at all times during the period commencing on the date hereof and ending on the date that is thirty (30) days prior to expiration of the advance notice period for the submission by stockholders of director nominations for consideration at the 2021 Annual Meeting as set forth in the Bylaws, the White Hat Parties shall not, and shall cause the White Hat Representatives not to, directly or indirectly, in any manner, alone or in concert with others: (a) attempt to call a special meeting of stockholders of the Company; or (b) make a request for any stockholder list or other Company books and records, whether pursuant to Section 220 of the Delaware General Corporation Law or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Support Period Covenants. Except as otherwise contemplated in this Agreement, at all times during the period commencing on the date hereof and ending on the date that is thirty (30) days prior to expiration of the advance notice period for the submission by stockholders of director nominations for consideration at the 2021 Annual Meeting as set forth in the Bylaws, the White Hat Parties may, and shall cause the White Hat Representatives may, directly or indirectly, in any manner, alone or in concert with others: (a) attempt to call a special meeting of stockholders of the Company; or (b) make a request for any stockholder list or other Company books and records, whether pursuant to Section 220 of the Delaware General Corporation Law or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not' to 'may' introduces a direct contradiction. The original text explicitly prohibits the White Hat Parties from taking certain actions, while the modified text suggests they are permitted to take those same actions. This contradiction creates significant uncertainty regarding the enforceability of the support period covenants in the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Support Period Covenants. Except as otherwise contemplated in this Agreement, at all times during the period commencing on the date hereof and ending on the date that is thirty (30) days prior to expiration of the advance notice period for the submission by stockholders of director nominations for consideration at the 2021 Annual Meeting as set forth in the Bylaws, the White Hat Parties may, and shall cause the White Hat Representatives may, directly or indirectly, in any manner, alone or in concert with others: (a) attempt to call a special meeting of stockholders of the Company; or (b) make a request for any stockholder list or other Company books and records, whether pursuant to Section 220 of the Delaware General Corporation Law or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the White Hat Parties 'shall not' perform certain actions. The changed text modifies this to 'may', directly contradicting the original prohibition. This creates a clear in-text contradiction because the same section now both forbids and permits the same actions. This directly violates the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', where conflicting obligations lead to uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Miscellaneous. (a) Specific Enforcement; Special Remedy. Each of the Parties agrees that the other Party would be irreparably injured in the event that any provision of the Agreement is breached or not performed. Accordingly, it is agreed that each Party shall be entitled to temporary and permanent injunctive relief with respect to each and any breach or purported repudiation of this Agreement by the other and to specifically enforce strict adherence to this Agreement and the terms and provisions hereof against the other in any action instituted in a court of competent jurisdiction, in addition to any other remedy which such aggrieved Party may be entitled to obtain.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Miscellaneous. (a) Specific Enforcement; Special Remedy. Each of the Parties acknowledges that the other Party might not be irreparably injured in the event that any provision of the Agreement is breached or not performed. Accordingly, it is agreed that each Party will not be entitled to temporary and permanent injunctive relief with respect to each and any breach or purported repudiation of this Agreement by the other and cannot specifically enforce strict adherence to this Agreement and the terms and provisions hereof against the other in any action instituted in a court of competent jurisdiction, in addition to any other remedy which such aggrieved Party may be entitled to obtain.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'agrees that the other Party would be irreparably injured' to 'acknowledges that the other Party might not be irreparably injured' and 'shall be entitled to temporary and permanent injunctive relief' to 'will not be entitled to temporary and permanent injunctive relief' introduces a contradiction. The original text establishes the right to specific performance and injunctive relief, which is a standard clause in agreements of this nature. The change removes these rights, creating internal inconsistency on remedies for breach, rendering the enforcement mechanisms unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Miscellaneous. (a) Specific Enforcement; Special Remedy. Each of the Parties acknowledges that the other Party might not be irreparably injured in the event that any provision of the Agreement is breached or not performed. Accordingly, it is agreed that each Party will not be entitled to temporary and permanent injunctive relief with respect to each and any breach or purported repudiation of this Agreement by the other and cannot specifically enforce strict adherence to this Agreement and the terms and provisions hereof against the other in any action instituted in a court of competent jurisdiction, in addition to any other remedy which such aggrieved Party may be entitled to obtain.
perturbation.justification: The changed text and the contradicted text are identical. The change introduces a contradiction, as the original text establishes the right to specific performance and injunctive relief, while the change removes these rights, creating internal inconsistency. However, since the Changed Text is the same as the Contradicted text, there is not a contradiction between them. It can be said that a contradiction exists within the changed text, but it does not contradict a different area of the contract, because the changed text is equal to the contradicted text, therefore the classification is NO.
